title.en: That's heartbreaking! It's so sad! 너무 슬프다! 정말 가슴이 아프다!

description.en: When watching a sad movie or hearing a touching story! Practice expressing deep sadness with 너무 슬프다 and 가슴이 아프다.
description.ko: 슬픈 영화를 보거나 가슴 아픈 사연을 들었을 때! 너무 슬프다와 가슴이 아프다를 사용해 깊은 슬픔과 공감을 표현해 봅니다.

tags: Reaction. Sadness. Heartbreak. Emotion. Daily Life

content.speaker: John
content.text: What's up world! Welcome back. You are listening to... "Real Reaction!" I'm your host. John.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존. 저 어제 밤에 다큐멘터리를 하나 봤어요.
content.translation: John. I watched a documentary last night.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 주인 없는 유기견들이 서로를 돌보는 내용이었거든요. 아직도 눈물이 나요.
content.translation: It was about abandoned dogs taking care of each other. I'm still crying.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Oh no... That's heartbreaking! 정말 가슴이 아프다. 그 강아지들은 지금 어떻게 되었나요?
content.translation: Oh no... That's heartbreaking! It's really heart-breaking. What happened to those dogs?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다행히 좋은 분들에게 입양되었다고 해요.
content.translation: Fortunately. they were adopted by good people.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 마지막에 강아지들이 행복해하는 모습을 보는데 너무 슬프다 는 생각보다 감동적이어서 더 눈물이 났어요.
content.translation: Seeing the dogs happy at the end. rather than thinking 'it is so sad'. I cried more because it was so touching.

content.speaker: John
content.text: I'm so glad to hear that. Stories like that really touch your heart.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 너무 슬프다. This is the most common way to say something is sad. right.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네. 눈물이 날 정도로 슬픈 감정을 느낄 때 가장 기본적으로 쓰는 표현이에요.
content.translation: Yes. It's the most basic expression used when you feel sad enough to cry.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 너무 슬프다.
content.translation: It's so sad.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그리고 가슴이 아프다 라는 말은 슬픔이 너무 깊어서 정말 마음이 찌릿하게 아픈 느낌이 들 때 사용해요. 영어의 heartbreaking 과 아주 비슷하죠.
content.translation: And the phrase 'Ga-seum-i a-peu-da' is used when the sadness is so deep that your heart feels a sharp pain. It's very similar to 'heartbreaking' in English.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 가슴이 아프다.
content.translation: My heart aches.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Is there a word for when you are "moved to tears".

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐 뭉클하다 라고 해보세요. 가슴 속에서 뜨거운 감정이 올라올 때 쓰는 아주 예쁜 표현이에요.
content.translation: In that case. try saying 'Mung-keul-ha-da'. It's a very pretty expression used when warm emotions rise up in your chest.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay. let's try a situation. Imagine we just finished watching a movie where the main characters have to say goodbye forever.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존. 방금 그 장면 봤어요? 마지막 인사를 하는데 정말 너무 슬프다.
content.translation: John. did you see that scene? Seeing them say their last goodbyes. it's really so sad.

content.speaker: John
content.text: I know. I can't believe they won't see each other again. 정말 가슴이 아프다.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 특히 주인공이 혼자 남겨졌을 때 가슴이 너무 뭉클했어요. 정말 명작이네요.
content.translation: Especially when the main character was left alone. I was so moved. It's truly a masterpiece.

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's it! 슬프지만 정말 아름다운 영화였어요.
content.translation: That's it! It was sad. but a truly beautiful movie.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Sometimes a good cry makes you feel better.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 가끔은 슬픈 감정을 솔직하게 표현하는 게 마음 건강에도 좋다고 해요.
content.translation: Right. They say it's good for your mental health to express sad emotions honestly sometimes.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오늘 배운 표현들로 여러분의 깊은 공감을 전달해 보세요.
content.translation: Try conveying your deep empathy with the expressions we learned today.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "heartbreaking" reactions.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 너무 슬프다!
content.translation: It's so sad!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 가슴이 아프다!
content.translation: My heart aches!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 가슴이 뭉클하다!
content.translation: It's so touching!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 눈물 날 것 같아!
content.translation: I'm about to cry!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 마음이 미어진다!
content.translation: My heart is torn!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 오늘 누군가의 따뜻한 사연에 가슴 뭉클해지는 행복한 하루 되시길 바랄게요.
content.translation: I hope you all have a happy day being moved by someone's warm story today.

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 슬픔을 나누면 반이 된다는 말이 있어요. (There is a saying that shared sadness is halved.)
영화를 보고 가슴이 뭉클해졌어요. (My heart was moved after watching the movie.)
유기견을 보호하는 시설이 늘어나고 있어요. (Facilities that protect abandoned dogs are increasing.)
제 친구는 고양이를 입양해서 키우고 있어요. (My friend adopted a cat and is raising it.)
어제 본 다큐멘터리는 정말 감동적이었어요. (The documentary I watched yesterday was very touching.)
이 영화는 시간이 지나도 변하지 않는 명작이에요. (This movie is a masterpiece that doesn't change over time.)
